---
title: "Lecture 16 Quiz: Arrow Functions & Callbacks"
jupyter: julia-1.12
---

::: {.callout-note}
Review before quiz:
[Lectures](/lectures/lecture-16.qmd) | [Week 4 Content](/content/week-04.qmd) | [Lab 4](/labs/lab-04.qmd) | [Assignment 2](/assignment/02-assignment.qmd)
:::

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
using QuizQuestions
```

## Lecture 16 Self-Check

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. `const f = (x) => x * 2;`", "B. `const f => x * 2;`", "C. `function => (x) x*2;`", "D. `const f = > (x) x*2;`"]
question = "Question 1: Which option is valid arrow function syntax?"
answer = 1
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. Function called only by browser", "B. Function passed to another function", "C. Function that returns object", "D. Function without parameters"]
question = "Question 2: A callback is:"
answer = 2
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. `map`", "B. `filter`", "C. `reduce`", "D. All of these"]
question = "Question 3: Which array methods commonly accept callback functions?"
answer = 4
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. `forEach` returns transformed array", "B. `map` returns transformed array", "C. `map` always mutates original array", "D. `filter` returns a number"]
question = "Question 4: Which statement about `map` is correct?"
answer = 2
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. Use arrow functions for every possible case", "B. Avoid callbacks entirely", "C. Keep callbacks small and readable", "D. Never use array methods"]
question = "Question 5: Which is a good practice when using callbacks?"
answer = 3
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```
